nodes.title: Explore the repo structure
nodes.description: Before changing anything, look around. Open the repo folder on your computer or on GitHub.com and understand what each folder is for. apps/ is for projects, docs/ is for business knowledge, workflows/ is for processes, journal/ is for notes.
nodes.tryIt: Open your repo and click through every folder. Read each README.md file inside them.

nodes.title: Configure your OS
nodes.description: A 5-question interview that personalizes your repo. It writes your name, project, goals, and communication preferences to config.json so your AI tools know who you are and how to help. No AI tool yet? Edit config.json directly on GitHub.com.
nodes.tryIt: Say to your AI tool: "Help me set up my OS" (or run /setup in Claude Code). Or edit config.json on GitHub.com -- fill in every field.

nodes.title: Build your operating manual
nodes.description: A 6-question interview about your business that generates docs/operating-manual.md. This document serves double duty: it's a real business reference for humans, and it's the context layer that makes every AI tool connected to your repo smarter. From this point on, AI knows what you do, how you work, and where you need help.
nodes.tryIt: Say to your AI tool: "Help me build my operating manual" (or run /build-manual in Claude Code). Answer the 6 questions. Review the generated doc and edit anything that doesn't sound right.

nodes.title: Make your first commit
nodes.description: Your repo now has real content. Committing is like saving a file, but smarter -- it records what changed and why. Ask your AI tool to commit for you, or use the three terminal commands: git add, git commit, git push. This is the core rhythm you'll use from now on.
nodes.tryIt: Say to your AI tool: "Commit my changes with the message: set up my OS and document how my business works". Or in a terminal: git add -A && git commit -m "set up my OS" && git push

nodes.title: Ask your AI tool something
nodes.description: Now that your AI tool has your config and operating manual, it has real context about your business. Ask it something useful. The AI reads your repo files automatically, so it already knows your goals, your workflow, and your pain points.
nodes.tryIt:
Try these prompts with your AI tool:
- "Help me plan [your 30-day goal]"
- "Brainstorm ideas to solve [your biggest pain point]"
- "Write an SOP for [something you do regularly]"

tracks.title: Start Operating
tracks.description: Use your OS for real work

nodes.title: Track a task with GitHub Issues
nodes.description: GitHub Issues is your built-in task tracker. Each issue is a task, idea, or question with its own discussion thread. Your repo comes with templates for ideas, tasks, and questions so you don't start from a blank page.
nodes.tryIt: Go to your repo on GitHub.com. Click the Issues tab, then 'New issue'. Pick the 'Task' template and create an issue for something you need to do this week.

nodes.title: Write a journal entry
nodes.description: The journal/ folder is your thinking space. Write daily notes, weekly reflections, ideas, lessons learned. The value builds over time: searchable, versioned, always accessible. Your AI tools can also read it for context on what you've been thinking about.
nodes.tryIt: Create a file called journal/week-1.md. Write 3-5 sentences about what you're working on this week and what you want to figure out. Commit it.

nodes.title: Log a decision
nodes.description: Six months from now you'll ask 'why did I do it this way?' The decision log answers that. Copy the template in docs/decisions/, fill it out for a real decision you've made recently. Context, decision, reasoning, consequences.
nodes.tryIt: Copy docs/decisions/000-template.md to docs/decisions/001-chose-github-os.md. Fill it out: why did you decide to use GitHub OS?

nodes.title: Document a process you repeat
nodes.description: If you do something more than twice, write it down. SOPs go in docs/sops/. Could be how you onboard a client, publish content, handle invoices, or run a weekly review. Written in markdown, versioned automatically, readable by AI.
nodes.tryIt: Say to your AI tool: "Help me write an SOP for [something I do regularly]. Save it to docs/sops/." Review the output, edit it, commit.

nodes.title: Get feedback on your work
nodes.description: Ask your AI tool to review anything: a document, a plan, a piece of writing. It gives structured feedback -- what's working, what to improve, and a suggested next step. Use it before you share or ship anything.
nodes.tryIt: Say to your AI tool: "Review my operating manual and give me feedback" (or run /review docs/operating-manual.md in Claude Code). Try it on your first SOP too.
